# public/data/

This directory contains static JSON files generated by the Python preprocessing pipeline.
These files are served directly by Next.js (no server-side Python at runtime).

## Files generated by the pipeline

| File | Generated by | Description |
|------|-------------|-------------|
| `movies.json` | `04_export_json.py` + `05_fetch_posters.py` | Full movie catalog with titles, genres, years, avg ratings, poster URLs |
| `tag_mood_map.json` | `02_preprocess.py` | Mood label → list of movieIds derived from real tags.csv keyword matching |
| `recs_{hash}.json` | `04_export_json.py` | Pre-computed SVD top-20 recommendations per seed profile (one file per genre×mood combo) |
| `profiles_manifest.json` | `04_export_json.py` | Maps profile hashes to {genres, mood} for debugging |
| `neighbor_data.json` | `04_export_json.py` | Per-movie neighbor rating distributions for explainability bar visualization |
| `poster_map.json` | `05_fetch_posters.py` | movieId → TMDB poster URL (used as a lookup cache) |

## Generating the data

Run the full pipeline in order:

```bash
cd /path/to/project
python scripts/01_download.py            # downloads ml-latest-small
python scripts/02_preprocess.py          # builds movies_enriched.json, tag_mood_map.json
python scripts/03_train_svd.py           # trains SVD, saves model.pkl
python scripts/04_export_json.py         # exports recs_*.json to this directory
TMDB_API_KEY=your_key python scripts/05_fetch_posters.py  # fetches poster URLs
```

The app falls back to content-based scoring if the JSON files are missing.
